title: carya ovata var. ovata
intro: Carya ovata var. ovata, also known as the Shagbark Hickory, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of ailments. The Shagbark Hickory has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The tree's bark can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine.
